name: Strategic Reasoning & Analysis Ontology
alternateName: REASON-ONT — MECE Trees, Logic Trees, Hypothesis-Driven Strategy & AI Analysis Synthesis
description: The AI agent reasoning substrate. Provides structured analytical patterns that give Gen AI agents rigorous frameworks for strategic thinking: MECE decomposition (structured problem breakdown), Logic Trees (quantitative metric driver analysis), Hypothesis-Driven Strategy (scientific method applied to strategy), and Analysis Synthesis (multi-framework convergence into recommendations). This is the cross-cutting meta-layer that structures HOW analysis is performed across all other VSOM-SA layers. The ontology graph becomes a reasoning substrate — not just data storage but a scaffold for AI-powered strategic analysis workflows. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SA (Strategy Analysis).
oaa:domain: Strategic Analysis, Decision Science, AI-Augmented Reasoning

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Question-to-Strategy AI Pipeline
oaa:joinPatterns.description: The complete AI agent strategic analysis pipeline: receive question, decompose via MECE, analyze per branch using frameworks, synthesize findings, update VSOM strategy. This is the E2E reasoning pattern.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: rsn:StrategicQuestion → rsn:decomposedBy → rsn:MECETree → rsn:hasBranch → rsn:MECEBranch [→ framework analysis per branch] → rsn:synthesizedInto → rsn:AnalysisSynthesis → rsn:updatesStrategy → vsom:VSOMFramework
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: AI agent performs structured end-to-end strategic analysis

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Hypothesis Validation Chain
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Scientific method for strategy: form hypothesis from strategic options, test against framework evidence, validate or invalidate, act accordingly.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: rsn:StrategicHypothesis → rsn:testedByAnalysis → [ind:SWOTAnalysis | ind:PortersFiveForcesAnalysis | bsc:BalancedScorecard | ...] → rsn:hasEvidence → rsn:StrategicHypothesis.status → rsn:hypothesisActioned → vsom:ObjectivesComponent

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Logic Tree Sensitivity to Strategy
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Quantitative driver decomposition identifies highest-sensitivity metric levers, which become strategic objectives in VSOM and BSC.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: rsn:LogicTree → rsn:hasLeafNode → rsn:LogicTreeNode [leaf with sensitivity] → rsn:leverMapsToKPI → kpi:KPI → kpi:trackedVia ← okr:KeyResult ← okr:Objective ← vsom:ObjectivesComponent
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Data-driven identification of highest-impact strategic levers

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Synthesis-to-BSC Operationalization
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Analysis synthesis recommendations cascade through VSOM into BSC perspective objectives for execution.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: rsn:AnalysisSynthesis → rsn:hasRecommendation → rsn:StrategicRecommendation → rsn:recommendationTargetsVSOM → vsom:StrategyComponent → bsc:operationalizedBy → bsc:BalancedScorecard → bsc:hasPerspective → bsc:BSCPerspective
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Analytical findings translate directly into balanced, measurable execution

oaa:joinPatterns.name: MECE Branch Framework Routing
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Each MECE branch is tagged with the most appropriate analysis framework, enabling AI agents to route each branch to the right analytical tool in parallel.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Parallel multi-framework analysis orchestrated by MECE decomposition

entities.name: StrategicQuestion
entities.description: A structured strategic question that drives an analysis cycle. Every strategic engagement starts with a question. The question type determines which VSOM layer is primarily addressed and which analytical frameworks are most relevant. Questions decompose into MECE trees, generate hypotheses, and connect to framework analyses. An AI agent receives a StrategicQuestion as its entry point.

oaa:properties.name: question
oaa:properties.description: The strategic question in natural language, e.g. 'How should we grow revenue 3x in 3 years?'

oaa:properties.name: questionType
oaa:properties.description: Classification of the strategic question type. Determines default MECE decomposition pattern and framework routing.

oaa:properties.name: vsomLayerTarget
oaa:properties.description: Which VSOM layer this question primarily addresses

oaa:properties.name: contextRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to orgctx:OrganizationContext @id

oaa:properties.name: sponsorRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id who needs the answer

oaa:properties.name: scope
oaa:properties.description: Scope of the question

oaa:properties.name: urgency
oaa:properties.description: Time pressure on the analysis

oaa:properties.name: constraints
oaa:properties.description: Known constraints (budget, time, regulatory, etc.)

oaa:properties.name: priorAnalysisRefs
oaa:properties.description: References to previous analyses that inform this question

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Current status of the analysis

oaa:properties.name: conclusion
oaa:properties.description: Final answer/recommendation — populated when status=Answered

oaa:properties.name: confidence
oaa:properties.description: Confidence in the conclusion (0.0-1.0)

oaa:properties.name: vsomActionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:StrategyComponent or vsom:ObjectivesComponent created as a result

entities.name: MECETree
entities.description: Mutually Exclusive, Collectively Exhaustive decomposition of a strategic question. The backbone of structured problem-solving. Each branch is independently analysable and together they cover the full solution space. MECE trees enable AI agents to parallelise analysis — each branch can be routed to a different framework and analyzed concurrently. Tree types determine default decomposition patterns: issue trees for diagnosis, hypothesis trees for validation, driver trees for quantification.

oaa:properties.name: questionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicQuestion @id being decomposed

oaa:properties.name: treeType
oaa:properties.description: Type of decomposition — determines structural pattern

oaa:properties.name: rootStatement
oaa:properties.description: The top-level statement being decomposed

oaa:properties.name: depth
oaa:properties.description: Maximum tree depth (typically 3-5 levels)

oaa:properties.name: branchCount
oaa:properties.description: Total number of branches (including sub-branches)

oaa:properties.name: leafNodeCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of leaf (terminal) nodes — these are the analysable units

oaa:properties.name: isMECE
oaa:properties.description: Validated as mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive

oaa:properties.name: meceValidationNotes
oaa:properties.description: Notes on MECE validation — gaps or overlaps identified

entities.name: MECEBranch
entities.description: A single branch (node) in a MECE tree. Can be an internal node (has sub-branches) or a leaf node (terminal, directly analysable). Each leaf branch is tagged with the most appropriate analysis framework and can carry findings. The branch structure enables AI agents to work top-down (decompose then analyse) or bottom-up (synthesise findings).

oaa:properties.name: label
oaa:properties.description: Short label for the branch

oaa:properties.name: level
oaa:properties.description: Depth level in the tree (0=root, 1=first level, etc.)

oaa:properties.name: parentBranchRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to parent rsn:MECEBranch @id (null for root)

oaa:properties.name: isLeaf
oaa:properties.description: True if this is a terminal node with no sub-branches

oaa:properties.name: position
oaa:properties.description: Order position among siblings

oaa:properties.name: assignedFramework
oaa:properties.description: Which analytical framework to apply at this branch. AI agents use this to route analysis.

oaa:properties.name: analysisRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to the specific analysis entity produced (e.g. ind:SWOTAnalysis @id, ind:PortersFiveForcesAnalysis @id)

oaa:properties.name: hypothesisRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicHypothesis @id tested at this branch

oaa:properties.name: finding
oaa:properties.description: Key finding from analysis at this branch

oaa:properties.name: evidenceStrength
oaa:properties.description: Strength of evidence supporting the finding

oaa:properties.name: valueImpact
oaa:properties.description: Estimated impact of this branch on the overall question

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Analysis status of this branch

entities.name: StrategicHypothesis
entities.description: A testable hypothesis about strategic direction. Hypothesis-driven strategy is the scientific method applied to business: (1) form hypotheses from VSOM strategic options, (2) identify critical assumptions, (3) design tests using framework analyses as evidence, (4) validate or invalidate each assumption, (5) update strategy confidence and direction. AI agents use this pattern to avoid confirmation bias — they must explicitly seek disconfirming evidence.

oaa:properties.name: statement
oaa:properties.description: The testable hypothesis statement, e.g. 'Entering the SME market segment will grow revenue by 40% within 18 months'

oaa:properties.name: questionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicQuestion @id this hypothesis addresses

oaa:properties.name: vsomStrategyRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:StrategyComponent @id being hypothesized

oaa:properties.name: hypothesisType
oaa:properties.description: Category of strategic hypothesis

oaa:properties.name: confidence
oaa:properties.description: Current confidence level (0.0-1.0) — updated as evidence accumulates

oaa:properties.name: riskIfWrong
oaa:properties.description: Consequence severity if this hypothesis proves wrong

oaa:properties.name: pivotDirection
oaa:properties.description: If invalidated: what alternative direction to explore

oaa:properties.name: vsomActionRef
oaa:properties.description: If validated: reference to vsom:ObjectivesComponent or vsom:StrategyComponent created

entities.name: HypothesisAssumption
entities.description: A critical assumption underlying a strategic hypothesis. Each assumption is independently testable. Assumptions are prioritised by criticality — 'must-be-true' assumptions are tested first because if they fail, the hypothesis is immediately invalidated. AI agents track assumption status to update overall hypothesis confidence.

oaa:properties.name: assumption
oaa:properties.description: The assumption statement

oaa:properties.name: criticality
oaa:properties.description: How critical this assumption is to the hypothesis

oaa:properties.name: testable
oaa:properties.description: Whether this assumption can be tested with available data/analysis

oaa:properties.name: testMethod
oaa:properties.description: How to test this assumption (e.g. 'market sizing analysis', 'competitor benchmark', 'customer survey')

oaa:properties.name: testFramework
oaa:properties.description: Which framework to use for testing

oaa:properties.name: analysisRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to the analysis entity providing evidence

oaa:properties.name: evidence
oaa:properties.description: Summary of evidence for/against this assumption

oaa:properties.name: confidenceInResult
oaa:properties.description: Confidence in the test result (0.0-1.0)

entities.name: EvidenceItem
entities.description: A single piece of evidence supporting or contradicting a strategic hypothesis. Evidence is gathered from framework analyses across VSOM-SA. Each item records source framework, finding, direction (supporting/contradicting), and strength. AI agents aggregate evidence items to compute hypothesis confidence scores.

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: What the evidence shows

oaa:properties.name: direction
oaa:properties.description: Whether this evidence supports or contradicts the hypothesis

oaa:properties.name: strength
oaa:properties.description: Strength of this evidence

oaa:properties.name: sourceAnalysisRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to specific analysis entity (e.g. ind:SWOTAnalysis @id)

oaa:properties.name: dataPoint
oaa:properties.description: Specific data point or metric, if quantitative

oaa:properties.name: reliability
oaa:properties.description: How reliable the underlying data is

entities.name: LogicTree
entities.description: Quantitative driver decomposition tree. Decomposes a business metric into its mathematical components (revenue = volume x price, etc.). Each leaf node maps to a kpi:KPI. Sensitivity analysis identifies which leaf-node levers have the highest impact on the root metric — these become strategic priority objectives in VSOM. Logic trees give AI agents the ability to answer: 'Which single lever would have the biggest impact on our target metric?'

oaa:properties.name: treeType
oaa:properties.description: Type of driver decomposition

oaa:properties.name: contextRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to orgctx:OrganizationContext @id

oaa:properties.name: questionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicQuestion @id if part of a larger analysis

oaa:properties.name: rootMetricName
oaa:properties.description: Name of the root metric being decomposed (e.g. 'Net Profit', 'Revenue', 'Customer LTV')

oaa:properties.name: rootKPIRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to kpi:KPI @id for the root metric

oaa:properties.name: rootUnit
oaa:properties.description: Unit of the root metric ($, %, #, etc.)

entities.name: LogicTreeNode
entities.description: A single node in a logic tree. Internal nodes define mathematical operators (add, subtract, multiply, divide) combining their children. Leaf nodes are the atomic levers — each maps to a kpi:KPI and carries sensitivity analysis showing its impact on the root metric. High-sensitivity leaf nodes become VSOM strategic objectives.

oaa:properties.name: parentNodeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to parent rsn:LogicTreeNode @id (null for root)

oaa:properties.name: operator
oaa:properties.description: Mathematical operator combining children (only for non-leaf nodes)

oaa:properties.name: kpiRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to kpi:KPI @id this node represents

oaa:properties.name: improvementLever
oaa:properties.description: For leaf nodes: what action would improve this metric

oaa:properties.name: sensitivityPct
oaa:properties.description: For leaf nodes: if this lever improves by 10%, how much does the root metric change (%)

oaa:properties.name: sensitivityRank
oaa:properties.description: Rank among all leaf nodes by sensitivity (1=highest impact)

oaa:properties.name: feasibility
oaa:properties.description: How feasible is it to move this lever

oaa:properties.name: vsomObjectiveRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:ObjectivesComponent @id if this lever becomes a strategic objective

oaa:properties.name: bscPerspective
oaa:properties.description: Which BSC perspective this lever maps to

entities.name: AnalysisSynthesis
entities.description: The synthesis layer where an AI agent combines findings from multiple framework analyses into coherent strategic recommendations. This is the 'so what?' — translating parallel analyses into convergent insights and actionable VSOM strategy updates. The synthesis identifies convergent findings (multiple frameworks agree — high confidence), divergent findings (frameworks disagree — requires resolution), and blind spots (areas not covered by any framework). The output is a set of StrategicRecommendations with evidence chains.

oaa:properties.name: questionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicQuestion @id being answered

oaa:properties.name: meceTreeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:MECETree @id structuring the analysis

oaa:properties.name: frameworksUsed
oaa:properties.description: List of framework types used in the analysis

oaa:properties.name: overallConfidence
oaa:properties.description: Overall confidence in the synthesis (0.0-1.0)

oaa:properties.name: strategicNarrative
oaa:properties.description: Coherent story connecting analyses to recommended strategic direction

oaa:properties.name: vsomFrameworkRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:VSOMFramework @id being updated

entities.name: SynthesisFinding
entities.description: A finding within the analysis synthesis — either convergent (multiple frameworks agree), divergent (frameworks disagree), or a blind spot (gap in analysis coverage). Convergent findings carry high confidence. Divergent findings require resolution narrative. Blind spots require additional analysis or explicit risk acceptance.

oaa:properties.name: supportingFrameworks
oaa:properties.description: Frameworks that support this finding

oaa:properties.name: contradictingFrameworks
oaa:properties.description: For divergent: frameworks that contradict

oaa:properties.name: resolution
oaa:properties.description: For divergent: how the contradiction was resolved

oaa:properties.name: confidence
oaa:properties.description: Confidence in this finding (0.0-1.0)

oaa:properties.name: strategicImplication
oaa:properties.description: What this finding means for strategy

entities.name: StrategicRecommendation
entities.description: An actionable strategic recommendation produced by the analysis synthesis. Each recommendation targets a specific VSOM layer, carries an evidence chain linking back to framework analyses, and has an assigned owner from RRR roles. Recommendations are the bridge from analysis to action — they become VSOM strategy updates, BSC objectives, OKR goals, or PPM initiatives.

oaa:properties.name: vsomLayerTarget
oaa:properties.description: Which VSOM layer this recommendation targets

oaa:properties.name: confidenceLevel
oaa:properties.description: Confidence in this recommendation (0.0-1.0)

oaa:properties.name: ownerRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id who should own execution

oaa:properties.name: bscPerspective
oaa:properties.description: Which BSC perspective this recommendation falls under

oaa:properties.name: evidenceChainRefs
oaa:properties.description: References to rsn:EvidenceItem @ids supporting this recommendation

oaa:properties.name: hypothesisRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicHypothesis @id this recommendation validates

oaa:properties.name: vsomComponentRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:StrategyComponent or vsom:ObjectivesComponent @id if accepted

oaa:properties.name: bscObjectiveRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to bsc:BSCObjective @id if operationalized

oaa:properties.name: ppmInitiativeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to ppm:Initiative @id if funded for execution

oaa:properties.name: gaRecommendationRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to ga:Recommendation @id if linked to gap analysis

relationships.name: Decomposed By MECE Tree
relationships.description: Strategic question is decomposed into a MECE tree structure

relationships.name: Has Branch
relationships.description: MECE tree contains branches

relationships.name: Has Sub-Branch
relationships.description: MECE branch contains sub-branches (recursive)

relationships.name: Generates Hypothesis
relationships.description: MECE branch generates a testable strategic hypothesis

relationships.name: Has Assumption
relationships.description: Strategic hypothesis has critical assumptions

relationships.name: Has Evidence
relationships.description: Strategic hypothesis has evidence items supporting or contradicting it

relationships.name: Tested By Analysis
relationships.description: Strategic hypothesis is tested by framework analyses from INDUSTRY-ONT, BSC-ONT, etc.

relationships.name: Hypothesis Actioned
relationships.description: Validated hypothesis produces VSOM action (strategy update or new objective)

relationships.name: Has Logic Tree Node
relationships.description: Logic tree contains nodes

relationships.name: Node Has Children
relationships.description: Logic tree node has child nodes (recursive)

relationships.name: Lever Maps To KPI
relationships.description: Logic tree leaf node maps to a KPI metric

relationships.name: Lever Becomes Objective
relationships.description: High-sensitivity logic tree lever becomes a VSOM strategic objective

relationships.name: Synthesized Into
relationships.description: MECE tree analysis results are synthesized into an AnalysisSynthesis

relationships.name: Has Synthesis Finding
relationships.description: Analysis synthesis contains findings (convergent, divergent, blind spots)

relationships.name: Has Recommendation
relationships.description: Analysis synthesis produces strategic recommendations

relationships.name: Updates Strategy
relationships.description: Analysis synthesis updates the VSOM framework with evidence-based recommendations

relationships.name: Recommendation Targets VSOM
relationships.description: Strategic recommendation targets a VSOM component for action

relationships.name: Recommendation Owned By
relationships.description: Strategic recommendation is owned by an executive role

relationships.name: Recommendation Funded By Initiative
relationships.description: Accepted recommendation is funded and executed via PPM initiative

relationships.name: Question Sponsored By
relationships.description: Strategic question is sponsored by an executive role

relationships.name: Question Triggers Review
relationships.description: Strategic question results can trigger a VSOM strategic review cycle

businessRules.name: Question Must Have VSOM Target
businessRules.action: THEN vsomLayerTarget MUST be specified to scope the analysis

businessRules.name: MECE Tree Must Be Validated
businessRules.condition: IF a MECETree is used for analysis
businessRules.action: THEN isMECE SHOULD be explicitly validated — gaps and overlaps must be documented in meceValidationNotes

businessRules.name: Leaf Branches Must Have Framework
businessRules.condition: IF a MECEBranch is a leaf node (isLeaf=true)
businessRules.action: THEN assignedFramework SHOULD be specified to enable AI agent routing

businessRules.name: Hypothesis Must Have Assumptions
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one HypothesisAssumption with criticality 'MustBeTrue'

businessRules.name: Must-Be-True Assumptions Tested First
businessRules.condition: IF a StrategicHypothesis has assumptions with criticality 'MustBeTrue'
businessRules.action: THEN those assumptions MUST be tested (status != 'Untested') before hypothesis status can move to 'Validated'

businessRules.name: Invalidated Assumption Invalidates Hypothesis
businessRules.condition: IF any HypothesisAssumption with criticality 'MustBeTrue' has status 'Invalidated'
businessRules.action: THEN the parent StrategicHypothesis status MUST be 'Invalidated' or 'Pivoted'

businessRules.name: Evidence Must Declare Direction
businessRules.action: THEN direction (Supporting/Contradicting/Neutral) MUST be explicitly declared to prevent confirmation bias

businessRules.name: Hypothesis Must Seek Contradicting Evidence
businessRules.condition: IF a StrategicHypothesis is being tested
businessRules.action: THEN the analysis SHOULD explicitly seek contradicting evidence — at least one EvidenceItem with direction 'Contradicting' or documented absence thereof

businessRules.name: Logic Tree Operator Required For Non-Leaf
businessRules.action: THEN operator MUST be specified (Add, Subtract, Multiply, or Divide)

businessRules.name: Logic Tree Leaf Must Map to KPI
businessRules.action: THEN kpiRef SHOULD reference a valid kpi:KPI @id for measurement

businessRules.name: High Sensitivity Levers Become Objectives
businessRules.condition: IF a LogicTreeNode has sensitivityRank <= 3 (top 3 levers)
businessRules.action: THEN vsomObjectiveRef SHOULD reference a vsom:ObjectivesComponent to ensure the highest-impact levers are strategically targeted

businessRules.name: Synthesis Must Reference Question
businessRules.action: THEN questionRef MUST reference the rsn:StrategicQuestion @id being answered

businessRules.name: Synthesis Must Identify Divergent Findings
businessRules.condition: IF an AnalysisSynthesis contains multiple framework inputs
businessRules.action: THEN any contradictions between frameworks MUST be documented as SynthesisFinding with findingType 'Divergent' and resolution provided

businessRules.name: Recommendation Must Have Evidence Chain
businessRules.condition: IF a StrategicRecommendation has priority 'Critical' or 'High'
businessRules.action: THEN evidenceChainRefs MUST reference at least one rsn:EvidenceItem @id

businessRules.name: Accepted Recommendation Must Target VSOM
businessRules.action: THEN vsomComponentRef MUST reference a valid vsom:StrategyComponent or vsom:ObjectivesComponent @id

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release of REASON-ONT as part of VE-Series VSOM-SA (Strategy Analysis). Provides the AI agent reasoning substrate: MECE decomposition, Logic Trees, Hypothesis-Driven Strategy with assumption tracking and evidence management, and Analysis Synthesis with convergent/divergent finding detection. Cross-references all VSOM-SA ontologies (BSC-ONT, INDUSTRY-ONT) plus VSOM-ONT, KPI-ONT, OKR-ONT, RRR-ONT, ORG-CONTEXT-ONT, GA-ONT, PPM-ONT. Key design principles: (1) explicit anti-confirmation-bias rules (BR-RSN-007/008), (2) assumption criticality tracking (BR-RSN-005/006), (3) evidence chain requirements for high-priority recommendations (BR-RSN-014).
